title: How would we help the Eva Trio Psychologically
display_title: How would we help the Eva Trio Psychologically
wikitext:
[]: How would we help the Eva Trio Psychologically?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Mar 18, 2014 10:48 pm It's no secret that a lot time we've spent on Evageeks or Evangelion in general is basically asking "What the hell is wrong with these kids that makes them so fucked up?" And, because of countless hours of debating and researching, I think we've finally come to a consensus of what makes these people who they are. Both Shinji and Asuka suffer from severe PTSD and abandonment issues. Shinji is a severe introvert that shuts himself out from reality to avoid pain and people hurting him. Asuka, of course, is the exact opposite, where she uses her massive ego and confidence as a barrier to protect herself from people hurting her as well. And, both of these unfortunately make the problem worse for both of them, as Shinji runs away from people, while Asuka pushes people away. Rei could also be a candidate, for she suffers from possibly Schizoid-Personality disorder and thus severely detaches herself from everything due to her upbringing. Now, instead of us arguing whether or not Rei is a "real person" because she has the soul of Lilith, let's argue at least for this thread's sake that she's a normal human being with problems. To us, it's obvious what types of problems plague each of the kids since we've analyzed them to death. But, in all the threads I've read in this forum, I've never come across the obvious next question: "How do we help them?" I think this would be a great question for some users who are planning to be psychologists and/or counselors. Think of it this way: What can we do to help them with their problems in a normal atmosphere, where psychological and pharmaceutical benefits are readily available for them? Let's say these kids lived in the real world, free from Nerv's watchful eyes and the constant threat of Angel attacks. What steps can we take to help these kids recover and learn to live normal, healthy lives? What medications are available that could help ease them into recovery? What do you guess think is the next step for these kids? :)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 18, 2014 11:46 pm Assuming this is all under normal circumstances: For Shinji, at least, I see his depression issues as situational with slight clinical (if that makes any sense) leanings. In other words, I don't necessarily think anti-depressants or anti-anxiety meds would do the trick, since they don't always for work for every person anyway. I'd say he could benefit from lots of talk therapy, if he's willing to work on changing himself for the better. Nobody can change the people you're around, the best you can do is change your own reaction to things. Rei and Asuka, I'm not really sure. But all 3 kids (hell, let's be real here, the whole damn cast except for the penguin!) could benefit from seeing a psychologist and/or psychiatrist, depending on how severe their issues are. One last thing to keep in mind is clinical disorders never truly go away (e.g. Depression), but there are at least tricks on how to make Depression/Anxiety not as severe the next time it rears its ugly head. My thoughts mostly come from my own personal experience (teenage and college years) of dealing with both clinical and situational Depression/Anxiety, so take it as you will.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Mar 19, 2014 12:04 pm I'd be Shinji's big brother, and support him whenever I could.

[]: Re: How would we help the Eva Trio Psychologically?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 19, 2014 2:14 pm xanderkh wrote: Both Shinji and Asuka suffer from severe PTSD and abandonment issues. Shinji is a severe introvert that shuts himself out from reality to avoid pain and people hurting him. Asuka, of course, is the exact opposite, where she uses her massive ego and confidence as a barrier to protect herself from people hurting her as well. And, both of these unfortunately make the problem worse for both of them, as Shinji runs away from people, while Asuka pushes people away.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Mar 19, 2014 3:30 pm Even as a person/actual human being, I don't know what you could with do with Rei and her set of problems. Much of her issues stem from her upbringing and conditioning, as well being too damn submissive. Hell, I think Shinji and Asuka could help her before they could help themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Mar 19, 2014 3:46 pm Really, all this psychological analysis aside, the only thing we'd be able to do, as human beings, is be emotionally supportive of them. Really, what Shinji needs is someone he feels safe being around; all Asuka needs is someone to give her validation as a person. All Rei needs (aside from removing her from her completely garbage situation) is some self-worth.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Mar 19, 2014 4:30 pm I would take Shinji to a house of ill repute.

[]:             Quote Post by dereliction_carnival » Thu Mar 20, 2014 1:42 pm They need a less alienating situation. Psychoanalysis? You mean that Freudian/Jungian superstitious mess used to shift pills? Prfff... Maslow had it right but his peers bullied him to hell and back for telling it like it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 20, 2014 4:29 pm You realize that those things have nothing to do with modern psychiatry, and that they (or rather, their modern descendants) are used by people who aren't in the business of peddling pills, right?

[]:             Quote Post by dereliction_carnival » Fri Mar 21, 2014 12:12 pm The DSM is a lot of superstition, modernised or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 21, 2014 1:06 pm dereliction_carnival wrote: The DSM is a lot of superstition , modernised or not.

[]:             Quote Post by dereliction_carnival » Fri Mar 21, 2014 1:19 pm Bagheera wrote: You keep using that word. I do not think it means what you think it means.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 21, 2014 2:02 pm dereliction_carnival wrote: "an irrational belief arising from ignorance or fear" sounds about right to me.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 21, 2014 2:11 pm Bags, watch the personal attacks man. Tone it down a notch. Carnival, it would be rather helpful if you'd explain your positions rather than just tossing out dismissive one-liners.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Mar 22, 2014 5:52 am Shinji needs someone to want him, Asuka needs someone to need her. The distinction is subtle, but important. Those are the baselines. Give them that and everything else will fall into place. Of course, as people are wont to remind me, indeed as Eva itself reminds us through Asuka's arc, external sources of meaning in life are shaky, and if the source is removed you're sent back to square one. So handle with care and don't say anything you don't mean.

[]:             Quote Post by dereliction_carnival » Sat Mar 22, 2014 10:07 am http://psychcentral.com/blog/archives/2013/05/07/did-the-nimh-withdraw-support-for-the-dsm-5-no/ The future will be neurology combined with therapy. This frees the therapy from having to be a system of classification and measurements made to fit the person. In other words personal therapy will become massive more irrational and random, while neurology will be liberated as a rapidly evolving technical authority. "Shinji needs someone to want him, Asuka needs someone to need her. The distinction is subtle, but important." Yes exactly. They're opposite sides of the same coin.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Mar 22, 2014 10:36 am dereliction_carnival wrote: http://psychcentral.com/blog/archives/2013/05/07/did-the-nimh-withdraw-support-for-the-dsm-5-no/

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Mar 22, 2014 2:30 pm CJD wrote: Shinji needs someone to want him, Asuka needs someone to need her. The distinction is subtle, but important.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Mar 22, 2014 2:51 pm Shinji is needed, but does not feel wanted. He knows that he is necessary to pilot the Eva because no one else can, but doesn't believe anyone wants him there or places any intrinsic value on him. His sole value is his utility as a pilot. His psychopathology comes from a feeling of rejected by his father. Asuka does not feel needed at all. She's built her entire identity around piloting but she constantly gets her ass kicked and the glory stolen by the rookie who doesn't want to be there. People often express want towards her (love letters, etc.) but it's not genuine, or not genuine to her. Her entire psychopathology comes from a feeling of being replaced. One fears rejection, the other, replacement. Hence Asuka's perpetual drive to create rivalries. Everyone is out to get her.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Mar 22, 2014 2:56 pm Okay, that's sensible. Just wasn't sure quite what the difference was from that brief line. ...though I'd have said it as "needs to be wanted" and "wants to be needed".

And, because of countless hours of debating and researching, I think we've finally come to a consensus of what makes these people who
